steps.title: Identify the key fact in the passage
steps.content: The passage states that “no source before 1155 includes references to the famous Round Table.” Preiddeu Annwfn was composed around 900 — well before 1155 — so it contains no Round Table references. Whatever conclusion the blank draws must follow from this timeline.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — '*Le Morte d'Arthur* is more historically accurate than *History*, because the *Mabinogion* had not been written when Geoffrey of Monmouth was writing his work.': Compares accuracy without grounding the claim in the Round Table timeline; the passage gives no basis for judging overall accuracy. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Malory did not use *Preiddeu Annwfn* as a source for information he presented about the Round Table.': Since Preiddeu Annwfn predates 1155 it contains no Round Table references, so Malory could not have used it as a Round Table source — a direct logical inference. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Geoffrey of Monmouth was unaware of stories of the Round Table when composing his *History*, though historians know that works containing such stories were available to him.': Claims awareness of Round Table stories before 1155, but the passage says no source before 1155 includes them, so no such works existed for Geoffrey. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Geoffrey of Monmouth's accounts of Arthurian legends in his *History* are more similar overall in content to the accounts in the *Mabinogion* than they are to the accounts in *Roman de Brut*.': Makes a content-similarity claim the passage never addresses. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
